title: I Call upon You with Folded Hands!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The original Tamil is reproduced in full below the translation and remains the authoritative text. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement. The OCR-derived text omitted the metadata date and rendered the printed closing date as `6-1 2075`; the source scan visibly prints 10 November 2013. It also corrupted the phrase `தேர்தல் நடைபெற` near the close. These readings are restored in the derived Tamil copy below. The canonical source files remain unchanged.
paragraphs: By-elections occur no matter which party is in power. But never in any by-election have the rules been thrown to the winds and lawlessness unleashed even before nominations opened, as is happening now under the AIADMK government. Nominations for the Yercaud constituency by-election can be filed only from 9 November 2013. Yet how many violations have already taken place there? Countless violations and excesses beyond every limit!

For our part, within two days of the election being announced, we invited all the party's frontline functionaries in Salem to Chennai. After hearing their views, we decided that the DMK would contest the by-election.

As soon as the party decided to field a DMK candidate in the Yercaud Assembly constituency by-election to be held on 4 December 2013, I personally wrote to the leaders of most political parties in Tamil Nadu formally requesting their support. I wrote:

> We have decided that the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam will contest the Yercaud (ST) Assembly constituency by-election scheduled for 4 December 2013. We believe it constructive to contest with the firm and united support of all opposition parties, thereby warning the ruling AIADMK against the anti-democratic actions it continues to pursue in Tamil Nadu. I affectionately request your party's support for the DMK candidate in this by-election, to help the party's decision to protect democracy in Tamil Nadu and defeat authoritarian thinking.
Many of those party leaders wrote back expressing support for the DMK candidate. Some said they would consult their national leadership and announce a decision. Some gave no reply.

Our one sorrow over this Yercaud by-election is that my dear Thambi, the “Lion of Salem,” Veerapandi Arumugam, is not here to lead it. I cannot help thinking how the work would have proceeded under his leadership, and how tirelessly he would have spun into action. What can we do? Can we defeat nature?

In his absence, Sivalingam, who is responsible for the district party organisation; party Election Work Secretary T. M. Selvaganapathy, MP; Youth Wing Deputy Secretary Rajendran; and Veerapandi's beloved son Raja have begun working systematically and effectively. Pon. Muthuramalingam has been appointed by party headquarters to support them closely, and he too has gone there and begun the by-election work.

Meanwhile, on 11 October 2013, interviews for candidate selection were held under my chairmanship at the party office in Anna Arivalayam. Only after calling and speaking with all forty-two Udanpirappugal who had applied to contest, and consulting the district's frontline party functionaries, did we announce Thiru V. Maran, MBA, as the party candidate.
Our candidate is young. He is a good man who can move energetically among the people and serve the constituency usefully. He is educated, cultured and warm in his dealings with others.

Thambi M. K. Stalin, the party Treasurer, and Thambi Duraimurugan, Deputy General Secretary, took part in the candidate-introduction meeting held on 15 October 2013. On returning, they described to me the extraordinary enthusiasm and strength of that meeting. Thambi Duraimurugan also attended the opening of the election office and has returned. In this Yercaud by-election, the DMK was the first party to conduct candidate interviews immediately and announce its candidate.

From the day he was announced, our candidate has continuously toured the entire constituency on foot, going from house to house, meeting voters directly and seeking their support. To remain there and carry out the party's election work without pause, headquarters has formed a nine-member “Yercaud Constituency Election Work Committee” comprising S. R. Sivalingam, T. M. Selvaganapathy MP, R. Rajendran, Veerapandi A. Raja, Aadhisankar MP, G. Sudamani, K. Sundaram, P. Gopal and C. Tamilselvan.

The AIADMK first formed an election committee of fifty-two members for the Yercaud by-election. It then decided that even they were not enough and added nine more, making sixty-one in all, perhaps after noticing that the digits add up to their lucky number seven! Every minister in the Cabinet has apparently invaded the constituency with an entire army and retinue. Reports say the state Secretariat in Chennai is empty. The very fact that they have appointed so many election coordinators shows how frightened they are of this by-election. Yet whether we can confront and withstand the lawlessness all of them together may unleash remains a serious question.
I now wish to draw your attention to what the ruling AIADMK is doing in the constituency with the cooperation of the district administration.

The Election Commission announced that the Model Code of Conduct applied only to the constituency. But officials, including the Salem District Collector, claimed it applied throughout the district. On that basis, without any prior notice or even informing the district DMK organisation, officials began removing DMK flagpoles across Salem district. Yet they feared to remove the ruling AIADMK's flagpoles and took no action against them.

Even after the District Collector announced that the Model Code was in force throughout Salem district from 5 October, the date the election was announced, free bicycles, laptops and other items were distributed to schoolchildren in many villages within Yercaud constituency, including Thumbal, Pappanaickenpatti and Vellalapatti. Were these distributed without the Collector's permission?

Similarly, after the Model Code came into force, the state government continued various welfare-scheme activities throughout Salem district, where Yercaud is situated. Salem district party in-charge Sivalingam complained to the Collector and senior district police officials, but the complaint was ignored and no action was taken. Chief Electoral Officer Thiru Praveen Kumar, IAS, however, received the petition and ordered that DMK flagpoles must not be removed anywhere in the district outside Yercaud constituency.

The Election Commission has ordered ministers visiting Salem district not to use government vehicles. Yet on 14 October 2013, Tamil Nadu's Health Minister travelled ostentatiously in a car bearing the state-government emblem and a red beacon. The party submitted a complaint, including a photograph, to the Election Commission.

On 28 October 2013, DMK Parliamentary Party leader T. R. Baalu met the Chief Election Commissioner of India in Delhi and submitted three petitions detailing how AIADMK ministers and the District Collector were misusing government machinery in violation of the Model Code during the Yercaud by-election.

Under the guise of launching new state buses, attempts were made to use public money and administrative machinery to paint the AIADMK symbol on those buses, implant it in the public mind and gain electoral advantage.

Through the Vazhapadi Village Administrative Officer, they fabricated a complaint against the DMK candidate, alleging that on 22 October 2013 he had travelled with ten vehicles in violation of the Model Code. A police officer himself told *The Hindu* that the complaint had been filed late, contained no evidence, gave none of the vehicle registration numbers and was unsupported by video.
Even *Dinamalar* reported on 30 October 2013 that a display compiling the AIADMK government's achievements had been placed in the Salem Collectorate complex for voters to see.

Jayalalithaa's portrait on houses built under the Green Houses Scheme in Yercaud constituency has not been covered. Her picture appears on mineral-water containers; government-funded advertising supports her; and her photographs are freely permitted everywhere. Hoardings proclaiming the AIADMK government's “two years of achievement” remain throughout the district, including at the Collector's office.

The party has complained that the Collector, instead of behaving impartially as an election officer, acts openly like an AIADMK district secretary. It is said that he is due to retire in a few months and is also related to an MLA belonging to an AIADMK ally.

This official, who is supposed to monitor and enforce the Model Code without the slightest deviation, has acted like the fence grazing upon the crop. At 10.45 a.m. on 6 November 2013, opposite the old court in Omalur, he conducted religious rituals and laid the foundation stone for a new Omalur Taluk office building costing Rs 2.52 crore. The Salem District Revenue Officer, Mettur Sub-Collector, Omalur Tahsildar, Block Development Officer and assistant executive engineers of the Public Works Department's Buildings division attended. When the district's highest official, charged with protecting the Model Code, treats it with contempt and violates it, to whom can one complain?

Only a few months ago, a case concerning this Collector came before the court, and I pointed it out then. Land acquisition for the Salem–Ulundurpet National Highway project took place under the DMK government in 2009. For widening 136 kilometres of highway, 19,000 acres of agricultural land were acquired. The Highways Department allotted Rs 250 crore as compensation. Farmers sought compensation at market value and filed a case in the Madras High Court.

The High Court directed the Salem and Villupuram Collectors to take appropriate action and pay proper compensation by December 2012. But no action was taken. The farmers alleged before the High Court itself that the Salem Collector was demanding a thirty per cent “commission.” The court censured the Collector in that case.

Thus, from the very beginning, the ruling party is preparing to face this election through brazen actions that are anti-democratic and contrary to law. More than twenty days remain before polling, yet how many excesses, acts of lawlessness and atrocities have already occurred? They have appointed every minister and party headquarters secretary to the election committee.

The DMK faces this by-election with the courage expressed in the words, “Though obstacles stand before us, we have mighty shoulders to overcome them.” I ask our district secretaries and former ministers to go there and contribute as much as they can to the party's election work. We cannot spend as the ruling party does. We must go to the voters, explain with evidence the errors, irregularities and anti-people actions of this government, and impress those facts upon their minds.

Party comrades must campaign peacefully for the Udanpirappu now in the field. They must ignore the violence and torrents of abuse that usually accompany by-elections, and not become obsessed with victory at any cost. They must work democratically, with the humanism emphasised by Thanthai Periyar, never forgetting the path of virtue and affection desired by Perarignar Anna and Perunthalaivar Kamarajar. They must win while upholding our culture.

When opponents attack and hurl degrading words, our comrades must endure them with fraternal affection. They must remember that Duty, Dignity and Discipline will ensure that we are never defeated in the field and can always stand with heads held high. I ask them to serve the people through the party. This is the appeal I make with emotion as one matured by experience:

> In the two ranks confronting one another in this by-election, examine, understand and decide who is the principled fighter who comes bearing the shield of victory with true ideological conviction and idealistic fervour; identify the camp of principle to which he belongs. When the fruit of victory is placed in his hands, and party workers stand humbly ready to receive it, recognise that “the rightful person has the right to receive what is rightfully his.” Remember with compassion that supporting him is the path of righteousness and the binding duty of everyone who cherishes democratic norms in politics. So that all may joyfully taste the fruit of democracy in this by-election, and so that our party candidate Thambi Maran may triumph, I call upon the great voters, with folded hands: come, come and perform your duty!
With affection,
M. K.
10 November 2013
